rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Stonestown Shopping Center, Lp For An Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of John P. Byrne
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 9, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LP Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Stonestown Shopping Center, LP For An Award Of Mandatory Attorneys Fees And Costs is granted in large part. The Court assumes that this motion is still in dispute notwithstanding the filing of a notice of settlement of the entire case on June 9, 2021 because the parties filed supplemental papers on this motion after that date. Based on that assumption, SSC is awarded $34,838.81 in attorneys' fees and $2,327.31 in costs. Section 20.2 of the parties' lease includes a fees and costs provision which provides for the award of fees and costs to the prevailing party in this case. SSC is the prevailing party. The fees and costs sought by SSC are adequately documented and the hourly rates and number of hours are reasonable. However, section 20.2 only covers contract claims. After a review of the court file, the court concludes that the fees should be apportioned two-thirds to the contract and one-third to the non-contract claims. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BEATRICE GISIE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 9, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action to the extent those causes of action are based on the allegation that the Assignment of Deed of Trust recorded on May 19, 2014 is fraudulent or otherwise invalid. All claims based on the allegation that the Assignment of Deed of Trust recorded on May 19, 2014 is fraudulent or otherwise invalid are time-barred. While plaintiff Beatrice Gisie was given leave to allege facts showing her discovery of the Assignment occurred within three years of her filing this action, she did not do so in her first amended complaint and it appears that she is unable to do so. Most or all of the arguments made by Ms. Gisie in opposition to this demurrer were previously rejected by the Court in ruling on the demurrer to the complaint and may not be relitigated in the context of this demurrer. However, because paragraph 29 of the first amended complaint appears to allege wrongful conduct based on the premise that the Assignment of Deed of Trust recorded on May 19, 2014 is valid and it cannot be determined as a matter of law that any claims based on the allegations in paragraph 29 are time-barred, Ms. Gisie is given one final opportunity to file an amended complaint within ten calendar days. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Gisie is given leave to allege one or more viable causes of action relating to or arising out of allegations in paragraph 29 of the first amended complaint provided that those claims do not allege and are not predicated on the allegation that Assignment of Deed of Trust recorded on May 19, 2014 is fraudulent or otherwise invalid. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: 2000 FOLSOM PARTNERS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. EAT JUST, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 9, 2021 line 5. DEFENDANT EAT JUST, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is Denied. Plaintiff's allegation of the amount owed as of March 1, 2021 is permissible and on its face does not violate the parties' lease.
